shows.title: Global Perspectives / Psychological Resistance
shows.guests: G. Edward Griffin, David H. Rosmarin
shows.first_half: First Half: Author and filmmaker G. Edward Griffin shares his insights and perspectives on global power structures, economic control, and historical conflicts, as well as the events happening on the planet right now.
shows.second_half: Second Half: David H. Rosmarin, PhD, will discuss why anxiety and worry are universal parts of the human experience in an uncertain world. He’ll explain how building psychological resilience and learning to work with anxiety, rather than resisting it or chasing an anxiety-free life, can lead to greater emotional strength and well-being.

shows.title: Challenging the Big Bang / Psychic Bloodlines
shows.first_half: First Half: Professor James McCanney will discuss what he believes is one of the most significant scientific developments of the past 50 years: the collapse of Big Bang cosmology. He’ll present ten scientific discoveries that he argues challenge the foundational assumptions of the Big Bang theory.
shows.second_half: Second Half: Medium Susanne J. Wilson will explore the concept of psychic bloodlines, examining whether paranormal abilities such as clairvoyance and prophecy may be inherited across generations. She’ll also discuss reports of contact with departed humans and non-human intelligences.

shows.title: Humanity's Purpose / Past Lives & Karma
shows.first_half: First Half: Music producer and metaphysical philosopher Steven Machat will explore humanity’s origins, purpose, and enduring existence. He’ll discuss concepts including the Creator, the Nephilim, humanity’s pursuit of knowledge and science, and the belief systems and guiding principles that shape our lives.
shows.second_half: Second Half: Therapist Holly Hall will present her research into reincarnation and past life regression, examining how past-life experiences may influence the present. She’ll also discuss the connections between astrology and karmic patterns, and the ways these forces may shape life as we know it.

shows.title: Gobekli Tepe & Karahan Tepe / UFO Disclosure
shows.first_half: First Half: Writer Laird Scranton will delve into the enigmatic Turkish archaeological sites of Göbekli Tepe and Karahan Tepe, which offer some of the earliest known evidence of organized civilization dating back roughly 11,000 years. He’ll explore what may have driven ancient hunter-gatherers to gather in this region and develop sophisticated megalithic stoneworking.
shows.second_half: Second Half: Stephen Bassett is a political activist pressing for formal disclosure of an extraterrestrial presence engaging the human race. He'll report on how the disclosure movement has picked up momentum, and will address the recently released government files.

shows.title: Life of Jimmy Chagra / Open Lines
shows.first_half: Writer Jack Sheehan joins guest host Rich Berra (email) to discuss the extraordinary life and criminal career of notorious marijuana smuggler Jimmy Chagra. Through persistence and a stroke of luck, Sheehan located Chagra living under an assumed identity in witness protection after he served 23 years in federal prison. Their conversation will explore Chagra’s rise in the drug trade, his legal battles, and the sensational case in which he was accused, but ultimately acquitted, of arranging the murder of Federal Judge John Wood. Followed by Open Lines. Give Rich a call and discuss whatever is on your mind!
